text.title: pair-programming Core Rules
text.summary: Seven testable rules including AI-navigator constraints.

rule.statement: In Strong-Style, an idea must go through the other person's hands to reach the computer. The navigator dictates; the driver types. The driver must understand before typing.
rule.rationale: Strong style forces explanation, catches mistakes, transfers knowledge.

rule.statement: When pairing with an AI agent as navigator, the human is always physically the driver. The agent emits ONE bounded instruction per turn, explains why, and waits for the human result.
rule.rationale: Keeps human in control; prevents the AI from running away with file-sized outputs.

rule.statement: Restate Strong-Style rules to the AI every ~10 turns. Under length pressure, agents revert to emitting full files.
rule.rationale: Drift is the default failure mode for AI navigators; periodic reset keeps behavior bounded.

rule.statement: Define the session mode at the start (classic, ping-pong, strong-style, tour-guide). Do not switch modes mid-session without resetting context.
rule.rationale: Mid-session mode switches confuse roles and lose retrospective context.

rule.statement: Use Pomodoro: 25-minute focused blocks, 5-minute breaks. Switch roles at breaks. Max 4-6 hours of pairing per day.
rule.rationale: Pairing is high-bandwidth and tiring; without pacing, quality collapses after 4 hours.

rule.statement: When working with an AI navigator, keep prompts short. Paste diffs and test output, not full files.
rule.rationale: Long context kills the navigator's focus and inflates costs.

rule.statement: Run a silent retro at the end of each session: agent generates a 5-bullet summary, human edits it. Persist to session.md.
rule.rationale: Captures decisions + state for the next session; otherwise context evaporates.

rule.statement: Mode fixed, roles right (human driver for AI navigator), Pomodoro running, session.md initialised.
Only one participant available, or task is routine parallelisable work.
